syntaxReference: Method declaration: public static int max(int a, int b) { return (a > b) ? a : b; }
Void method (no return): private static void printTitle(String title) { System.out.println(title); }
Calling a helper: int m = max(x, y);
Pre/Post in comments: // requires: n >= 0\n// ensures: returns sum 0..n
Using constants: private static final int MAX_TRIES = 5;
Overloading example: int abs(int x) { ... } double abs(double x) { ... }
Early return to simplify logic: if (!valid) return; // do rest
Composing helpers: String cleaned = normalize(input); List<String> words = tokenize(cleaned);
commonPatterns: Top-down outline: main -> readInput(); process(); report();
Loop with helper condition: while (!done(state)) { state = step(state); }
Validation pipeline: isValid = checkA(x) && checkB(x) && checkC(x);
Sentinel input: int n = readIntWithSentinel(sc, -1); while (n != -1) { ...; n = readIntWithSentinel(sc, -1); }
quickTips: Name methods by intent (what) not implementation (how).
Prefer passing data as parameters over reading globals inside helpers.
Refactor duplication into a helper the second time you see it.
